test cases.title: Successful User Registration with Valid Email and Password
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter a unique, valid email address in the 'Email' field (e.g., 'newuser@example.com').
Enter a strong password meeting complexity requirements in the 'Password' field (e.g., 'P@ssw0rd123').
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
test cases.expectedResult: User account is successfully created. User is redirected to the dashboard/home screen, or a success message is displayed.

test cases.title: Attempt to Register with Already Registered Email (Email/Password)
preconditions: An account 'existing@example.com' already exists (registered via Email/Password).
User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter 'existing@example.com' in the 'Email' field.
Enter a valid password in the 'Password' field.
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
test cases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Email already registered' or 'Account already exists' is displayed.

test cases.title: Attempt to Register with Email Linked to Google SSO Account
preconditions: An account 'googleuser@example.com' already exists (registered via Google SSO).
User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter 'googleuser@example.com' in the 'Email' field.
Enter a valid password in the 'Password' field.
Tap the 'Register' or 'Sign Up' button.
test cases.expectedResult: The system provides clear guidance, either prompting the user to log in with Google, offering to link the accounts, or indicating the email is already in use via Google SSO.

test cases.title: Network Interruption during Registration
preconditions: User is on the Registration screen.
Device is connected to the internet.
steps: Enter valid registration details.
Tap 'Register' button.
Immediately disable network connectivity (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi/cellular data) before the request completes.
test cases.expectedResult: An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Network error', 'Unable to connect'). User data is not lost, and the registration process can be retried once connectivity is restored.

test cases.title: Successful First-Time Login via Google SSO (New User)
preconditions: User is on the Login/Register screen.
User has a valid Google account not yet linked to the application.
steps: Tap the 'Login with Google' or 'Sign Up with Google' button.
Select a Google account or enter Google credentials.
Grant necessary permissions when prompted by Google (if applicable).
test cases.expectedResult: A new user account is successfully created and linked to the Google account. User is redirected to the dashboard/home screen.

test cases.title: Successful Subsequent Login via Google SSO (Existing Linked User)
preconditions: An existing user account is already linked to a Google account.
User is on the Login/Register screen.
steps: Tap the 'Login with Google' button.
Select the previously linked Google account.
test cases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home screen.

test cases.title: User Cancels Google SSO Flow
preconditions: User is on the Login/Register screen.
steps: Tap the 'Login with Google' button.
When prompted by the Google authentication interface, tap 'Cancel' or close the popup/webview.
test cases.expectedResult: Google SSO flow is aborted. User remains on the Login/Register screen without logging in, and no error message is displayed from the application side.

test cases.title: Google Service Unavailable during SSO Attempt
preconditions: User is on the Login/Register screen.
Simulate Google authentication service being unavailable or returning an error.
steps: Tap the 'Login with Google' button.
test cases.expectedResult: An error message indicating an issue with Google services or generic login failure is displayed to the user.

test cases.title: Network Interruption during Google SSO Handshake
preconditions: User is on the Login/Register screen.
Device is connected to the internet.
steps: Tap the 'Login with Google' button.
During the Google authentication process (e.g., after selecting account but before redirect back to app), disable network connectivity.
test cases.expectedResult: The Google SSO flow fails. An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Network error', 'Unable to complete login'). User remains on the Login/Register screen.

test cases.title: Successful Forgot Password Request and Reset
preconditions: An account 'existing@example.com' exists and is registered via Email/Password.
User is on the Login screen.
steps: Tap 'Forgot Password?' link.
Enter 'existing@example.com' in the 'Email' field.
Tap 'Send Reset Link' or similar button.
Access the registered email account and click the password reset link.
On the reset password page/in-app prompt, enter a new strong password meeting complexity requirements.
Confirm the new password and tap 'Reset Password'.
test cases.expectedResult: A confirmation message for the reset link request is displayed. The email receives a valid reset link. Password is successfully updated, and user can log in with the new password.

test cases.title: Request Forgot Password for Unregistered Email
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' screen.
steps: Enter an email address not registered in the system (e.g., 'unknown@example.com') in the 'Email' field.
Tap 'Send Reset Link' or similar button.
test cases.expectedResult: A message indicating that an email has been sent (to avoid leaking account existence) or a generic 'email not found' error (less secure, but depends on policy) is displayed. No reset email is sent.

test cases.title: Attempt to Use Expired Password Reset Link
preconditions: An account 'existing@example.com' exists.
A password reset link for 'existing@example.com' has been generated and has expired (e.g., after a configured timeout or another reset request was initiated).
steps: Click the expired password reset link.
test cases.expectedResult: The user is redirected to an error page or an in-app message stating 'Link expired' or 'Invalid link'. Prompt to request a new reset link.

test cases.title: Attempt to Reset Password with Invalid New Password (Complexity)
preconditions: User has accessed a valid password reset form via a reset link.
steps: Enter a password that does not meet complexity requirements (e.g., 'short' or 'password123') in the 'New Password' field.
Confirm the same invalid password.
Tap 'Reset Password'.
test cases.expectedResult: An error message indicating 'Password does not meet complexity requirements' is displayed.

test cases.title: Network Interruption during Forgot Password Request
preconditions: User is on the 'Forgot Password' screen.
Device is connected to the internet.
steps: Enter a valid email address.
Tap 'Send Reset Link'.
Immediately disable network connectivity before the request completes.
test cases.expectedResult: An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Network error', 'Unable to connect'). User remains on the 'Forgot Password' screen.

test cases.title: Email Input Validation - Invalid Formats
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password screen, with email input field active.
steps: Enter 'user' in the email field.
Attempt to submit or move to next field.
Enter 'user@domain' in the email field.
Attempt to submit or move to next field.
Enter 'user@.com' in the email field.
Attempt to submit or move to next field.
Enter 'user@domain.c' in the email field.
Attempt to submit or move to next field.
Enter 'user with space@domain.com' in the email field.
Attempt to submit or move to next field.
test cases.expectedResult: An immediate, clear error message 'Invalid email format' or similar is displayed for each invalid entry. The system prevents submission until a valid format is entered.

test cases.title: Email Input Validation - Leading/Trailing Spaces
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Forgot Password screen, with email input field active.
steps: Enter ' user@example.com ' (with leading and trailing spaces) in the email field.
Attempt to submit.
test cases.expectedResult: The system should trim leading/trailing spaces and validate the email address correctly. If not trimmed, it should reject it as invalid or show an error.

test cases.title: Password Complexity Validation - Insufficient Length
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Password Reset screen, with password input field active (assuming min length > 1).
steps: Enter 'abc' (less than assumed minimum length, e.g., 8 characters) in the password field.
Attempt to submit.
test cases.expectedResult: An immediate, clear error message 'Password must be at least X characters long' is displayed. Submission is prevented.

test cases.title: Password Complexity Validation - Missing Character Types
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Password Reset screen, with password input field active (assuming complexity includes uppercase, lowercase, number, special character).
steps: Enter 'passwordword' (only lowercase, meets length) in the password field.
Attempt to submit.
Enter 'Password123' (missing special char) in the password field.
Attempt to submit.
test cases.expectedResult: An immediate, clear error message indicating missing complexity requirements (e.g., 'Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character') is displayed. Submission is prevented.

test cases.title: Password Complexity Validation - Copy-pasting Malformed Input
preconditions: User is on the Registration or Password Reset screen, with password input field active.
steps: Copy a very long string or a string with unusual characters (e.g., emojis if not supported) from clipboard.
Paste it into the password field.
Attempt to submit.
test cases.expectedResult: The input field should either sanitize/truncate or reject the input, and display an appropriate validation error message (e.g., 'Password exceeds maximum length', 'Invalid characters in password').

test cases.title: App Background/Foreground during Login/Registration Flow
preconditions: User initiates a registration or login (e.g., after entering details or during SSO redirect).
steps: Start registration/login flow.
Put the app into the background (e.g., navigate to home screen or another app).
Wait for a few seconds/minutes.
Bring the app back to the foreground.
test cases.expectedResult: The app should resume the flow correctly from where it left off, or gracefully reset to the initial Login/Register screen without data loss or unexpected errors. SSO flows should handle redirects correctly.

test cases.title: Device Orientation Change during LR Page Interaction
preconditions: User is on the Login/Register page.
steps: Rotate the device from portrait to landscape (or vice-versa).
Verify UI elements are correctly rearranged/displayed.
Enter some input in a field, then rotate again.
test cases.expectedResult: The UI should adapt smoothly to the new orientation without layout issues or data loss in input fields. All elements should remain visible and interactive.

test cases.title: Login Performance under Normal Network Conditions
preconditions: User has a stable Wi-Fi/4G connection.
An existing Email/Password user account.
steps: Open the app to the Login screen.
Enter valid email and password.
Tap 'Login'.
test cases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in. The total time taken from tapping 'Login' to dashboard/home screen fully loaded is less than 3 seconds.

test cases.title: Registration Performance under Normal Network Conditions
preconditions: User has a stable Wi-Fi/4G connection.
User is on the Registration screen.
steps: Enter a unique valid email and a strong password.
Tap 'Register'.
test cases.expectedResult: User account is successfully created and redirected to the dashboard/home screen. The total time taken from tapping 'Register' to dashboard/home screen fully loaded is less than 3 seconds.
